course_description:
With the advent of social media platforms, social data has been produced at an unprecedented rate in the last decade, offering organizations new avenues for data-driven value creation and capture. This course explores how organizations can develop a social media analytics capability to better listen, understand and interact with their customers in real-time. At the technical level, students will be introduced to concepts and methods of network analysis and natural language processing and will be able to use contemporary software tools to access, analyse and visualise real-world social network data.

In the era of Web 2.0 and beyond, the rapid growth of social media platforms has fundamentally transformed the way individuals and businesses operate. Social media users continuously generate vast amounts of data through interactions, content sharing, and online engagement. These digital footprints create unprecedented opportunities for organisations to better understand customers, markets, and social behaviour.

As businesses increasingly rely on social networks for marketing, customer engagement, brand management, and strategic decision-making, there is a growing demand for business analysts who can extract actionable insights from complex social data and translate them into competitive advantage. This course provides an interactive blend of social media analytics, business strategy, and graph-based data mining, offering a practical foundation for understanding both the fundamentals and strategic potential of social analytics in business contexts.

Specifically, the course covers:

By developing these skills, students will be able to identify online communities with shared interests, design data-driven recommendation strategies, support targeted marketing campaigns, and analyse how information spreads across networks.

The course also integrates real-world case studies of successful social media strategies, combined with hands-on exercises and interactive discussions. This approach ensures students gain both technical capability and strategic insight into the evolving role of social analytics in modern organisations.

Course Changes in Response to Previous Student Feedback

course_aims: This course aims to endow students with the sense of how business insights can be grasped from the large amount of social media data, and to enable students to obtain skills to wrangle, analyze, and visualize the graph-structured data generated by social platforms.ﾠ

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate an understanding of advanced techniques for collecting, processing, and analysing social media data for different business applications.
Critically evaluate and effectively communicate the outcomes of social media analysis to inform, generate, and justify strategic recommendations for business stakeholders.
Actively apply skills using current and emerging tools in social analytics to tackle new business challenges and gain insights.

assessment_details.title: Milestone 1: Project Proposal
assessment_details.task_description: This project aims to design and implement an effective recommender system, which performs social analytics on the interaction network of users and products, and then recommends products to target users. The main task in this assessment is to deliver a formal project proposal outliningthe proposed plansfor achieving the project goal.AI StatementArtificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.Read full task descriptionHide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You may be able toapply for an extension.
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
Apenaltyof 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Milestone 2: Project Report and Presentation
assessment_details.task_description: The main task in this assignment is to deliver a final project report and a presentation detailingthe execution, methodology, and outcomesof the project proposed in Assignment 1.Algorithm-generated resultsfor users in the test set are to be submitted. We will evaluate your submitted results based on the Recall@10 metric (averaged across users) and corresponding marks will be given based on your algorithm’s accuracy.AI StatementArtificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task.
